author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
avivekarūpa ghana, tāhe dik ācchādana,
haila tā’te andhakāra ghora
tāhe duḥkha⋅vṛṣṭi haya, dekhi’ cāridike bhaya,
pathabhrama hoiyāche mora

nija aviveka⋅doṣe, paḍi’ durdinera roṣe,
prāṇa jāya saṁsāra⋅kāntāre
pathapradarśaka nāi, e durdaive mārā jāi,
ḍāki tāi, acyuta, tomāre

ekabāra kṛpādṛṣṭi, kara āmā⋅prati vṛṣṭi,
tabe mora ghucibe durdina
viveka sabala ha’be, e bhaktivinode tabe,
dekhāibe patha samīcīna
en_translation:
O Hari! Clouds of ignorance cover the directions, creating dense darkness. Misery rains down, and I see fear on all sides. I have lost my way.

By my own lack of discrimination and by the force of misfortune, my life is being lost in the forest of the material world. There is no guide, and in this great calamity, I am dying. Therefore, I call out to You, Acyuta!

Please bestow just one merciful glance upon me. Then my days of misfortune will end. My discrimination will become strong, and then Bhaktivinoda will be shown the proper path.
